title: App Distribution
objectives: Ship pre-release builds to testers fast

sections.markdown: Firebase App Distribution ships APK/AAB (Android) and IPA (iOS) builds to invited testers by email — no Play/App Store review. Great for daily internal builds and QA loops.

sections.code:
# Upload from CI
firebase appdistribution:distribute app-release.apk \
  --app 1:1234:android:abcd \
  --release-notes-file notes.txt \
  --groups qa,internal

sections.markdown: The typical setup: **daily builds → App Distribution** for internal QA, **weekly builds → Play Internal / TestFlight** for wider beta, **release → production tracks**. This lets designers/QA test hot changes without waiting for review.
Automate via `fastlane`, `codemagic`, or GitHub Actions. Include commit SHA + build number in release notes. Set the auto-update SDK so testers get notified in-app when a new build lands.

flashcards.back: APK/AAB/IPA to invited testers by email, without store review.
Fastest iteration for internal QA (both platforms); TestFlight for larger iOS beta programs.

quiz.q: In a typical release pipeline it's used for…
quiz.explain: Fast iteration to a small tester group.

challenges.text: Upload one APK to a tester group via CLI.
Automate distribution from GitHub Actions on push to main.
Add in-app update prompt using the App Distribution Testers SDK.
Multi-stage pipeline: App Distribution (nightly) → Play internal (weekly) → production track.

interviewQuestions: When use App Distribution vs Play Internal vs TestFlight?
How do testers get notified of new builds?
How do you deprecate old testers?
